name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is a nationally recognized short-term lender offering payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans ($100–$1,000), and title loans up to $25,000 at this Jacksonville, FL location.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized, fully accredited consumer lending company that has served millions of Americans seeking short-term financial relief. The company operates hundreds of physical store locations across the United States, including the Jacksonville, Florida branch at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave. in the Commonwealth Village neighborhood — positioned west of downtown between the I-95 and I-295 corridors, just north of I-10. As a company, Advance America markets itself as a trusted, fast source of emergency personal loans for consumers who need funds quickly and may not qualify for traditional bank financing.

This Jacksonville location offers three primary lending products plus money transfer services. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are designed as short-term two-to-four-week cash bridges between paychecks, available both online and in-store. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment through multiple scheduled payments over time, offering larger amounts and longer terms than payday products. Title Loans, offered through a partnership with LoanCenter, can reach $2,000 to $25,000 online, with loan amounts based on the appraised value of a fully paid-off vehicle held as collateral. Western Union money transfer services round out the product lineup. Customers can apply online, by phone for pre-qualification, or by visiting the store in person.

Advance America carries one of the largest customer review bases of any short-term lender, with a 4.9 out of 5 star rating across 125,401 verified reviews and more than 200,000 total reviews on Google — an unusually strong and consistent satisfaction signal for this category. The Jacksonville location provides bilingual service in Spanish, with staff noted in customer reviews for thoroughly explaining the loan process rather than rushing applicants. Extended hours Monday through Saturday, including early opens on Fridays and Saturdays, combined with online and phone pre-qualification channels, make the service accessible for working customers with limited weekday availability.

Advance America fills a real credit gap for consumers who face urgent cash needs and lack access to lower-cost financing. However, prospective borrowers should understand the cost structure clearly. Payday loans typically carry triple-digit APRs when annualized, and repeated rollovers or re-borrowing can compound financial stress rather than relieve it. Installment loans offer more structured repayment but still carry rates well above what banks or credit unions charge to qualified borrowers. Title loans carry an additional and serious risk: failure to repay can result in vehicle repossession, which may create further hardship for borrowers who depend on their car for income. These products are best treated as occasional emergency tools, not routine credit facilities.
address: 509 N State of Franklin Rd #1, Johnson City, TN 37604

company_info.headquarters: 509 N State of Franklin Rd #1, Johnson City, TN 37604

pros: 4.9/5 star rating across 125,401 customer reviews — unusually high and consistent satisfaction for the short-term lending category
Three loan types under one roof: payday, installment, and title loans covering $100 to $25,000
Title loans up to $25,000 via LoanCenter partnership — substantially higher ceiling than payday-only lenders
Bilingual English/Spanish staff available in-store for Spanish-speaking customers
Multiple application channels: online, phone pre-qualification, and in-store walk-in
Western Union money transfer services available at this location
Saturday hours (9am–4pm) for customers unable to visit on weekdays
cons: Payday loan maximum of $500 is insufficient for larger emergency expenses
Title loans require a fully paid-off vehicle with clear title in the applicant's name — excludes most borrowers
Closed on Sundays, limiting access for true weekend emergencies
Checking account required to qualify — excludes unbanked consumers from payday and installment products
Short-term loan APRs are consistent with the broader payday lending industry, meaning triple-digit annualized rates that make repeated borrowing expensive
best_for: Consumers needing $100–$500 quickly to bridge a gap until their next paycheck
Borrowers who need up to $1,000 and prefer spreading repayment across multiple installments rather than a lump-sum payoff
Vehicle owners with a fully paid-off car who need $2,000–$25,000 and understand the collateral risk
Spanish-speaking customers seeking bilingual loan service in the Jacksonville area
diagnosis: Best for consumers with immediate, short-term cash needs up to $1,000 who have exhausted lower-cost options such as credit union PALs or employer advances, or for vehicle owners who need larger sums through title lending. The primary caveat is cost: payday and installment loan APRs are high by industry standard, and title loans carry real repossession risk — these products should only be used for genuine one-time emergencies with a clear repayment path already in place.
services: Payday Loans ($100–$500, 2–4 week term, online or in-store)
Installment Loans ($100–$1,000, multi-payment schedule, online or in-store)
Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000 online, via LoanCenter partnership)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
